name: shire-autocompletion
description: Fast inline FIM code autocomplete powered by your own OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Qwen2.5-Coder, Codestral, DeepSeek-Coder...). Recent-edit refactor assist + cross-file repo context.

shire.baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL (must end at /v1). The extension calls {baseUrl}/completions. Point this at your own server (vLLM, llama.cpp, Ollama, TGI, or any hosted endpoint).

shire.apiKey.description: API key sent as Authorization: Bearer. Leave empty for local servers that need none.

shire.model.description: Completion model name as registered on your endpoint. Use a FIM-capable code model (Qwen2.5-Coder, Codestral, DeepSeek-Coder, StarCoder2).

shire.debounceMs.description: Idle time after the last keystroke before a request fires.

shire.maxPrefixChars.description: Max characters of code before the cursor sent as FIM prefix.

shire.maxSuffixChars.description: Max characters of code after the cursor sent as FIM suffix.

shire.temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Low = more deterministic completions.

shire.multiline.description: Allow multi-line completions. Turn off for single-line-only.

shire.enableRecentEdits.description: Feed your recent before/after edits into the prompt so the model propagates similar changes across files (refactor assist).

shire.enableRepoContext.description: Index the workspace and inject the most relevant cross-file snippets into completions. Lexical (BM25) retrieval, no extra latency on the hot path.

shire.enableSemanticRetrieval.description: Also use embeddings (your embedModel) for similarity retrieval. Higher relevance but adds a query-embedding network round-trip. Off by default for latency.

shire.embedModel.description: Embedding model name on your endpoint, used for the repo index when semantic retrieval is on.

shire.maxNeighborFiles.description: Max cross-file snippets injected per completion.

shire.retrievalTimeoutMs.description: Hard cap on retrieval time. If retrieval is slower, the completion proceeds without cross-file context rather than waiting.

shire.embedConcurrency.description: How many files to embed in parallel when building the semantic index. Higher = faster build but more load on your embedding endpoint.

shire.maxIndexFiles.description: Max files to index in LEXICAL mode (semantic off). Handles large monorepos. If the repo has more, the set is sorted by path and capped deterministically.

shire.maxSemanticFiles.description: Max files to index when SEMANTIC retrieval is on (every file is embedded, so this is kept lower than maxIndexFiles). Sorted-by-path deterministic cap.
